hotels.name: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
hotels.notes: 5 min walk from Shinjuku Station South Exit. Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane) 2 min walk.
hotels.taqbin_note: Send large bags to Fujiya Hotel, Hakone this morning — travel with daypack only
hotels.map_query: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku Tokyo

hotels.name: Fujiya Hotel (est. 1878)
hotels.notes: Historic hotel est. 1878. Yukata and geta sandals provided. Orchid Room restaurant. 3 min walk from Miyanoshita Station.

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
hotels.notes: 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street. Free late-night ramen served 9:30–11 PM nightly.

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX
hotels.onsen_type: Rooftop natural hot spring + city views
hotels.notes: Lawson convenience store attached to hotel. Free late-night ramen 9:30–11 PM. Open til midnight.
hotels.taqbin_note: Send large bags to Residence Hotel Takayama this morning — bags arrive May 22
hotels.map_query: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX Osaka

hotels.name: Residence Hotel Takayama Station
hotels.notes: 30 seconds from Takayama Station! Full apartment with kitchen. Large bags arriving from Osaka on May 22.

hotels.name: Yuen Shinjuku
hotels.onsen_type: Rooftop indoor + outdoor onsen with Shinjuku skyline
hotels.notes: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station. Rooftop onsen open late — best around 11 PM for Shinjuku skyline views.

trains.seat_tip: Activate JR Pass at Narita JR counter BEFORE boarding. Platform B1.
trains.booking_note: Covered by JR Pass — activate at Narita on arrival

trains.seat_tip: Sit RIGHT side — Mt Fuji visible at the 40 min mark. Seat 1A or 1B for panoramic front view.
trains.booking_note: Book at odakyu.jp at least 2 weeks ahead — NOT covered by JR Pass

trains.seat_tip: 🥇 SIT RIGHT SIDE D seats! Mt Fuji appears around 10:20 AM — LAST Fuji view from this direction! Gran Class: fully reclining seats, meal + drinks + hot towel + amenity kit, cabin attendant.
trains.booking_note: JR Pass covers base fare, Gran Class supplement required — reserve at any JR office on arrival in Japan

trains.seat_tip: Shortest Shinkansen of the trip! Covered by JR Pass.
trains.booking_note: Covered by JR Pass — reserve seat at any JR office

trains.seat_tip: Sit LEFT side A/B seats — Hida River gorge views appear 90 min in. Turquoise river + alpine villages + dramatic cliffs!
trains.booking_note: Covered by JR Pass — reserve LEFT side seats at any JR office

trains.seat_tip: Sit LEFT side for gorge views on the return journey too.
🌿 SIT RIGHT SIDE D/E seats! FINAL Mt Fuji view around 12:20 PM between Shin-Fuji and Mishima stations — very last Fuji sighting of the trip! Green Car: wider seats, more legroom, quieter cabin.
trains.booking_note: JR Pass covers base fare, Green Car supplement required — reserve at Nagoya Station JR office on May 23

trains.seat_tip: Last train ride in Japan! Return Suica cards at Narita JR office to get remaining balance + ¥500 deposit refund.
trains.booking_note: Covered by JR Pass — departs 2:20 PM, do NOT miss this train!

onsens.description: Historic Fujiya Hotel mineral pools — the hotel has been drawing guests to these baths since 1878. Mineral-rich mountain water, classic Japanese architecture, peaceful mountain setting.
onsens.tips: Yukata and geta sandals provided by hotel. Main bath floor. Try the outdoor rotenburo under the mountain sky.

onsens.description: Dawn soak in Fujiya Hotel's rotenburo — almost no other guests at this hour. Watch the Hakone mountains emerge from morning mist while soaking in mineral-rich water.
onsens.tips: Go at 6:30 AM — nearly empty! The early start today is worth it for this experience.

onsens.description: Tenzan Tohji-kyo — a beloved public onsen in a cedar forest by the Hayakawa River. Multiple pools at different temperatures, outdoor riverside rotenburo surrounded by ancient cedar trees.
onsens.tips: One of Hakone's most atmospheric public onsens. Open late. Separate male/female sections.

onsens.description: Last soak at Fujiya Hotel before departing for Kyoto. Say goodbye to the Hakone mountains from the rotenburo — check-out is at 8:45 AM so this is the final chance.
onsens.tips: Pack bags the night before so you can enjoy this final soak fully. Last memory of Hakone.

onsens.type: Rooftop natural hot spring + city views
onsens.description: Natural hot spring on the rooftop of Dormy Inn Namba — soak above the Osaka neon lights after a big day of Dotonbori, Glico Man, takoyaki and bar-hopping.
onsens.tips: Open until midnight. Natural hot spring water pumped up from below. City lights + hot spring = perfect combo. Free ramen in hotel restaurant 9:30–11 PM before you head up.

onsens.type: Rooftop indoor + outdoor onsen — Tokyo skyline
onsens.description: Yuen Shinjuku's rooftop onsen with the full Shinjuku skyscraper skyline as the backdrop. Indoor and outdoor pools, relaxation area. The city view at night from an outdoor hot spring is genuinely breathtaking.
onsens.tips: Go around 11 PM when quieter. Available on both May 23 and May 24 nights. This is the final onsen of the trip — savour it!

itinerary.title: Arrive Tokyo

activities.name: Land at Narita Airport (NRT)
activities.description: Flight arrives

activities.name: Immigration, baggage claim, customs
activities.description: Allow 60–90 min

activities.name: Pick up 2× Suica cards at JR ticket counter
activities.description: Narita Terminal 1 ground floor. Also activate JR Passes here!

activities.name: Board N'EX Narita Express to Shinjuku
activities.description: Platform B1 — 90 min first views of Japan!

activities.name: Arrive Shinjuku Station — South Exit
activities.description: 5 min walk to hotel

activities.name: Check in — JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
activities.map_query: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku Tokyo

activities.name: Dinner — Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane)
activities.description: Smoky yakitori alley — 2 min walk from hotel. Grab a stool at any tiny bar!

activities.name: Kabukicho + Golden Gai evening stroll
activities.description: 200 tiny bars — each fits 6–8 people, just walk in and sit. Very safe and very local.

activities.name: Back to hotel — early night (jet lag!)

tips: Activate your JR Pass at the Narita JR counter before boarding N'EX — show your exchange order.
Golden Gai: each bar fits 6–8 people, just walk in and sit. Very safe and very local.
Omoide Yokocho: point at whatever looks good on the grill — it always works!

itinerary.title: Kimono + Asakusa + Shibuya + teamLab

activities.name: Depart hotel
Kimono Rental Wargo — Asakusa branch
activities.description: Choose pattern beforehand at wargokyoto.com. Book online in advance!

activities.name: Senso-ji Temple + Nakamise shopping street
activities.description: Tokyo's most famous temple — iconic Thunder Gate (Kaminarimon)

activities.name: Tsukiji Outer Market — graze lunch
activities.description: Fresh seafood, tamagoyaki, street snacks — arrive hungry!

activities.name: Meiji Jingu Shrine — forested approach path
activities.description: Tranquil forested shrine in the heart of Tokyo

activities.name: Takeshita Street — kawaii fashion and crepes
activities.description: Wild street fashion, colourful crepes, Japanese pop culture

activities.name: Hachiko Statue + Shibuya Scramble Crossing
activities.description: The world's busiest pedestrian crossing — up to 3,000 people cross at once!

activities.name: Starbucks 2nd floor — aerial view of crossing
activities.description: Best free seat in Tokyo — watch the scramble from above

activities.name: Dinner — Ichiran Ramen Shibuya (solo booth style)
activities.description: Each person gets their own private booth. Customize your ramen on a form!

activities.name: teamLab Planets Toyosu — immersive digital art
activities.description: Walk through water rooms and infinity mirror installations. Wear slip-on shoes — water rooms!

activities.name: Return to Shinjuku

tips: Book Kimono Rental Wargo online at wargokyoto.com — choose pattern beforehand.
Book teamLab Planets at teamlab.art — sells out weeks ahead!
Wear slip-on shoes for easy on/off at kimono fitting and teamLab water rooms.

itinerary.title: Shibuya Sky + Sumo Natsu Basho

activities.name: Depart hotel
Shibuya Sky — observation deck floors 45 & 46
activities.description: 360° views, open-air rooftop, Mt Fuji visible to the west on clear mornings

activities.name: Travel to Ryogoku — sumo town
Chanko nabe pre-sumo lunch at Tomoegata
activities.description: The protein stew sumo wrestlers eat — at a restaurant run by former sumo stable

activities.name: Collect pre-booked sumo tickets at will-call
activities.description: Book in advance at sumo.or.jp — sells out!

activities.name: Browse sumo souvenir stalls outside arena
activities.description: Sumo goods, wrestler face fans, commemorative towels

activities.name: Enter Ryogoku Kokugikan — grab arena bento
activities.description: May Natsu Basho — top division bouts run 2–6 PM

activities.name: Makuuchi top division bouts — Yokozuna bout ~5:45 PM!
activities.description: Watch wrestling at the highest level — the grand champion bout is the climax!

activities.name: Dinner — Chanko Dining Wakamatsu
activities.description: Another sumo restaurant near arena — different style from lunch

activities.name: JR Sobu Line back to Shinjuku
activities.description: 30 min

activities.name: Optional — Omoide Yokocho drinks
activities.description: 2 min from hotel

tips: Book Shibuya Sky at shibuya-scramble-square.com — 10:30 AM slot for best Mt Fuji visibility.
May Natsu Basho runs May 11–25 — perfect timing!
Book sumo tickets at sumo.or.jp well in advance — they sell out fast.

itinerary.title: Romance Car → Hakone + Open Air Museum + Fujiya Hotel

activities.name: TA-Q-BIN bags forwarded to Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: ⚠️ LUGGAGE DAY — Send large bags to Fujiya Hotel via hotel front desk. Arrives afternoon.

activities.name: Check out JR Hotel — daypack only
Odakyu Romance Car — Shinjuku → Hakone-Yumoto
activities.description: 85 min scenic mountain journey. Sit RIGHT side for Mt Fuji at 40 min mark!

activities.name: Arrive Hakone-Yumoto — buy 2-day Hakone Free Pass
activities.description: Station kiosk — covers ALL Hakone transport (trains, ropeway, buses, pirate ship)

activities.name: Ashiyu free foot baths at Hayakawa riverside
activities.description: Free foot onsen along the river — dip tired feet in hot spring water!

activities.name: Hakone Tozan Railway → Miyanoshita
activities.description: 15 min switchback mountain railway — zigzags up the mountain!

activities.name: Arrive Fujiya Hotel — drop bags at front desk
activities.description: 3 min walk from Miyanoshita Station

activities.name: Lunch — Fujiya Hotel Orchid Room
activities.description: Historic chandeliers and mountain views. Try the Western course menu!

activities.name: Tozan Railway → Chokoku-no-Mori (Open Air Museum)
activities.description: 10 min

activities.name: Hakone Open Air Museum — sculptures + Picasso + foot onsen!
activities.description: Don't miss the foot onsen inside — soak while looking at sculptures and Hakone mountains!

activities.name: Proper check in — yukata and geta sandals provided!
activities.description: Change into yukata for the authentic ryokan-style evening

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 1 — Fujiya Hotel historic mineral pools
activities.description: Main bath floor in hotel. Indoor + outdoor rotenburo. Try the outdoor bath!

activities.name: Dinner — Fujiya Hotel Orchid Room
activities.description: Try Western course — served since 1878! Legendary historic dining.

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 1 continued — second evening soak
activities.description: Hotel baths quieter now

tips: Book Romance Car at odakyu.jp at least 2 weeks ahead — seat 1A or 1B gives panoramic front view.
Open Air Museum: don't miss the foot onsen inside — soak while looking at sculptures!
Tomorrow is earliest start of trip (6:15 AM) — set alarm tonight!

itinerary.title: Kawaguchiko + Mt Fuji + Owakudani — EARLY START 6:15 AM

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 2 — dawn rotenburo at Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: Hotel baths almost empty at dawn — watch Hakone mountains emerge from morning mist

activities.name: Quick breakfast — 7-Eleven or Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: Light breakfast to get moving for Mt Fuji!

activities.name: Tozan Railway → Hakone-Yumoto
activities.description: 15 min

activities.name: Fujikyuko Highway Bus → Kawaguchiko
activities.description: From Hakone-Yumoto — 90 min scenic ride. Mt Fuji is clearest before 11 AM!

activities.name: Buy Kawaguchiko Retro Bus Pass (1-day)
activities.description: At Kawaguchiko station kiosk — covers all retro bus routes

activities.name: Climb 398 stone steps to Chureito Pagoda
activities.description: Red Line bus to Shimo-Yoshida then 5 min walk

activities.name: 🗻 CHUREITO PAGODA — iconic Mt Fuji view!
activities.description: BUCKET LIST! Five-story pagoda framing Mt Fuji perfectly. Stand LEFT of pagoda for classic shot with Fuji centered.

activities.name: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway (Kachi Kachi Yama)
activities.description: Cable car up for aerial views of Lake Kawaguchi with Mt Fuji behind
activities.transport: Red Line bus to ropeway base

activities.name: Ring the love bell at summit together 🔔
activities.description: Summit shrine — couples tradition! Ring the bell for good fortune.

activities.name: Lunch — Kawaguchiko lakefront hoto noodles
activities.description: Hoto: flat udon noodles in miso broth with root vegetables — local specialty!

activities.name: Lake Kawaguchi stroll + optional swan boat
activities.description: Peaceful lakefront with Mt Fuji reflections on calm days

activities.name: Fujikyuko Bus back to Hakone-Yumoto
activities.description: 90 min return journey

activities.name: Hakone Shrine + Peace Torii Gate on Lake Ashi
activities.description: Dramatic torii gate rising from the lake — best at dusk
activities.map_query: Hakone Shrine Torii Gate Lake Ashi

activities.name: Lake Ashi rowboat rental — 30 min
activities.description: Row on the lake with Mt Fuji (if visible) and Hakone Shrine as backdrop

activities.name: Pirate Ship — Hakone-machi to Togendai
activities.description: 30 min lake cruise on a pirate ship! Covered by Hakone Free Pass.

activities.name: Hakone Ropeway → Owakudani volcanic valley
activities.description: 15 min aerial cable car over volcanic landscape

activities.name: Owakudani — sulfur vents + kuro tamago black eggs!
activities.description: Eggs hard-boiled in volcanic hot springs turn black. Legend: eating one adds 7 years to your life!

activities.name: Ropeway → Cable Car → Railway back to Miyanoshita
activities.description: 40 min total. Last ropeway down ~9 PM in May.

activities.name: Late dinner — Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: In hotel

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 2 continued — final Fujiya Hotel soak
activities.description: Hotel baths

tips: Check fujisan.jp the night before for Mt Fuji visibility — May mornings are excellent!
Chureito photo tip: stand LEFT of pagoda for classic shot with Fuji centered behind all five stories.
Last ropeway down from Owakudani is around 9 PM in May — our 8:15 PM arrival gives 45 minutes.

itinerary.title: Hakone → Gran Class Shinkansen → Kyoto Arrival

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 3 — final dawn rotenburo at Fujiya Hotel
activities.description: Last Hakone onsen — make it count!

activities.name: Final Fujiya Hotel breakfast — Orchid Room
activities.description: Savour it!

activities.name: Check out Fujiya Hotel — thank the staff warmly
Tozan Railway → Hakone-Yumoto → Odawara
activities.description: 30 min total

activities.name: Reserve Gran Class seats at JR ticket office
activities.description: Show JR Pass, pay Gran Class supplement. Only 18 seats per train — reserve ASAP!

activities.name: 🥇 GRAN CLASS Hikari Shinkansen — Odawara → Kyoto
activities.description: ~2 hours. SIT RIGHT SIDE D seats! Fully reclining seats, meal + drinks + hot towel + amenity kit + cabin attendant.

activities.name: 🗻 Mt Fuji appears on RIGHT side!
activities.description: Between Odawara and Shin-Fuji stations — LAST Fuji view from this direction for the whole trip!

activities.name: Arrive Kyoto Station
Lunch — Kyoto Ramen Koji (10th floor of station)
activities.description: 8 different ramen restaurants under one roof — try Kyoto-style with chicken broth

activities.name: Taxi to Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
activities.description: 10–15 min from Kyoto Station

activities.name: Check in Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
activities.description: Gion area — 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street

activities.name: Taxi to Higashiyama District
activities.description: 15 min

activities.name: Kiyomizu-dera Temple
activities.description: UNESCO World Heritage — famous wooden stage jutting out over the hillside, city views

activities.name: Sannenzaka + Ninenzaka historic stone-paved lanes
activities.description: Beautifully preserved Edo-period lanes lined with tea houses and craft shops

activities.name: Yasaka Shrine at dusk — stone lanterns glowing
activities.description: Lanterns light up beautifully at dusk — walk slowly through the grounds

activities.name: Gion — Hanamikoji Street — spot maiko 6:30–7:30 PM!
activities.description: Walk slowly on Hanamikoji. No flash photography. Bow respectfully if a maiko passes.

activities.name: Dinner in Gion area
activities.description: Multiple options along Gion lanes

activities.name: Taxi back to hotel
activities.description: 10 min

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 4 — Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion hotel onsen
activities.description: Unwind after a big travel day

tips: Gran Class has only 18 seats per train — reserve as early as possible at Odawara JR office!
D seats on right side for Mt Fuji — this is the last Fuji view from this angle at around 10:20 AM.
Maiko spotting: 6–7:30 PM on Hanamikoji — walk slowly, no flash photography, bow respectfully.

itinerary.title: Arashiyama + Tea Ceremony + Nijo Castle + Samurai Museum

activities.name: Taxi to Kyoto Station
activities.description: 10 min

activities.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove — walk deeper than 100m!
activities.description: Most famous bamboo grove in Japan — the deeper you go, the more magical it gets

activities.name: Tenryu-ji Zen Garden — UNESCO, designed 1339
activities.description: One of Japan's greatest zen gardens, with borrowed scenery of Arashiyama mountains

activities.name: Lunch in Arashiyama
activities.description: Shigetsu vegetarian (in Tenryu-ji) or Yoshimura soba

activities.name: Oi River walk + Togetsukyo Bridge
activities.description: Peaceful riverside walk — famous bridge framed by forested mountains

activities.name: JR Sagano → Kyoto Station → Karasuma Subway
activities.description: 25 min + 5 min

activities.name: Camellia Tea Ceremony — 90 min authentic experience
activities.description: Full authentic matcha tea ceremony in English. Wear comfortable clothes you can sit in.

activities.name: Nijo Castle — nightingale floors! UNESCO Heritage
activities.description: Walk on the famous squeaking floorboards — designed to alert guards of intruders

activities.name: Gold-leaf interiors + chirping floorboards + gardens
activities.description: Walk as slowly and quietly as possible — the chirping is subtle but unmistakable!

activities.name: Samurai and Ninja Museum — armor + sword + shuriken
activities.description: Interactive museum — try on real samurai armor, handle katana, throw shuriken!

activities.name: Dinner — Pontocho kawadoko riverside terrace
activities.description: Riverside terrace dining only available May–September. Perfect timing!

activities.name: Kamo River evening walk — sit on the stone steps
activities.description: Locals sit along the Kamo River in warm evenings — join them!

activities.name: ♨️ Dormy Inn Kyoto onsen
activities.description: In hotel

tips: Book Camellia Tea Ceremony at camellia-teaceremony.com — English sessions fill fast!
Nijo nightingale floors: walk as slowly and quietly as possible — the chirping is subtle but unmistakable.
Pontocho kawadoko terrace is only available May–September — perfect timing!

itinerary.title: Fushimi Inari + Nishiki Market + Kinkaku-ji + Farewell Gion

activities.name: Taxi to Inari Station
activities.description: 10 min

activities.name: Fushimi Inari — climb to Yotsutsuji + beyond!
activities.description: Go PAST Yotsutsuji — most tourists miss the best upper section! Allow minimum 1.5 hours.

activities.name: JR Nara Line → Kyoto Station
activities.description: 5 min

activities.name: Nishiki Market — 130 stalls, graze everything!
activities.description: Kyoto's kitchen — tofu donuts, pickles, matcha, fresh seafood, grilled skewers. Arrive hungry!

activities.name: Kinkaku-ji — the Golden Pavilion
activities.description: Temple covered in actual gold leaf — shimmers in afternoon light. 2:20 PM timing is perfect!

activities.name: Sekkatei teahouse matcha + exit matcha soft serve
activities.description: Have matcha at the teahouse inside, then get matcha soft serve at the exit gate

activities.name: Taxi to Gion area
activities.description: 20 min

activities.name: Gion antique shopping — Shinmonzen + Furumonzen
activities.description: Buy Kyoyaki ceramics on Shinmonzen Street — genuine world-class quality

activities.name: Maruyama Park — weeping cherry + locals picnicking
activities.description: Locals relaxing under cherry trees — peaceful park in the heart of Gion

activities.name: Yasaka Shrine at dusk — lanterns glowing
Hanamikoji Street — farewell Gion walk
activities.description: Last evening stroll through Gion before moving on to Osaka

activities.name: FAREWELL KYOTO DINNER — Pontocho kawadoko
activities.description: Final Kyoto dinner on the riverside terrace

activities.name: Final Kamo River walk — sit on the steps
activities.description: 2 min walk

activities.name: Taxi back to hotel — pack for Osaka
activities.description: 10 min. Pack tonight!

activities.name: ♨️ Dormy Inn onsen — final Kyoto soak
activities.description: In hotel

tips: Fushimi Inari: go PAST Yotsutsuji — most tourists miss the best upper section! Allow minimum 1.5 hours.
Kinkaku-ji: gold leaf shimmers in afternoon light — 2:20 PM timing is perfect.
Buy Kyoyaki ceramics on Shinmonzen Street — genuine world-class quality.

itinerary.title: Kyoto → Osaka + Castle + Kuromon + Dotonbori

activities.name: Check out Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
Taxi to Kyoto Station
activities.description: 10 min

activities.name: Hikari Shinkansen Kyoto → Shin-Osaka
activities.description: 15 min — shortest Shinkansen of the trip!

activities.name: Taxi to Dormy Inn Namba ANNEX — drop bags
activities.description: 15 min from Shin-Osaka
activities.map_query: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX Osaka

activities.name: Taxi to Osaka Castle
activities.description: 15 min

activities.name: Osaka Castle outer grounds — free walk
activities.description: Giant stone walls, moat, English signage throughout

activities.name: Lunch in castle park
activities.description: Grill Kapitan or park picnic

activities.name: Osaka Castle main tower — 8 floors + observation deck
activities.description: Museum inside + 360° city views + gold tiger displays

activities.name: JR Osaka Loop Line → Namba
activities.description: 20 min

activities.name: Check in Dormy Inn Namba ANNEX
activities.description: Lawson convenience store attached!

activities.name: Kuromon Ichiba Market — Osaka's Kitchen!
activities.description: 170 stalls — fresh seafood, wagyu beef skewers, takoyaki, crab. Graze your way through!

activities.name: Amerikamura — Triangle Park, BIG STEP, vintage
activities.description: Osaka's hip district — vintage fashion, streetwear, art, cafes

activities.name: Shinsaibashi Shopping Arcade + Don Quijote
activities.description: Covered shopping arcade + the legendary Don Quijote store (open 24h)

activities.name: Dotonbori at dusk — neon canal reflections!
activities.description: Stand on Ebisu Bridge facing west — Glico Running Man on left, full neon canal strip behind you

activities.name: Glico Running Man photo — mandatory couple photo!
activities.description: Ebisu Bridge by canal — face west for the neon backdrop

activities.name: Takoyaki at Ichifuji — the original and most authentic
activities.description: Not the flashiest stall but the best flavor. The real deal!

activities.name: Dinner — Kani Doraku (giant mechanical crab!)
activities.description: Canalside — king crab course meal under the famous giant mechanical crab sign

activities.name: Bar hopping — Bar K whisky + Craft Beer Base Kamikaze
activities.description: Namba backstreets

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 5 — Dormy Inn rooftop natural hot spring
activities.description: Natural hot spring + city views — open til midnight!

activities.name: Free late night ramen — Dormy Inn signature!
activities.description: Hotel restaurant — served 9:30–11 PM nightly. Do NOT miss it!

tips: Dotonbori photo: stand on Ebisu Bridge at dusk facing west — Glico Running Man on left, full neon canal strip behind you.
Takoyaki: Ichifuji is the most authentic — not the flashiest stall but the best flavor.
Dormy Inn free ramen served 9:30–11 PM nightly — do not miss it!

itinerary.title: Den Den Town + Shinsekai → Wide View Hida → Takayama

activities.name: ⚠️ TA-Q-BIN bags to Residence Hotel Takayama
activities.description: LUGGAGE DAY — Send large bags to Takayama via hotel front desk. Bags arrive May 22!

activities.name: Check out Dormy Inn Namba — daypack only
Breakfast — Lawson attached to hotel
activities.description: Ground floor — quick and easy

activities.name: Den Den Town — Osaka's Akihabara (Nipponbashi)
activities.description: Electronics, retro games, anime, figures — Osaka-style

activities.name: Joshin, Rocket retro games, Animate, Figure King
activities.description: Browse retro games, anime figures, electronics

activities.name: Walk to Shinsekai district
activities.description: 15 min walk — retro 1920s district under Tsutenkaku Tower

activities.name: Shinsekai — Jan Jan Yokocho retro arcade
activities.description: Retro atmosphere, old-school entertainment, billiards, shogi

activities.name: Fugu (puffer fish) breakfast at standing restaurant
activities.description: Licensed chefs, fully safe. Try the Osaka specialty before leaving!

activities.name: Subway to Osaka Station
activities.description: Dobutsuen-mae > Namba > Osaka

activities.name: Hikari Shinkansen Osaka → Nagoya
activities.description: 50 min

activities.name: Quick lunch — Nagoya ekiben (try miso katsu bento!)
activities.description: Station platform shops at Nagoya — Nagoya specialty miso katsu!

activities.name: Wide View Hida Ltd. Express — Nagoya → Takayama
activities.description: LEFT side A/B seats! Hida River gorge appears 90 min in — turquoise river, alpine villages, dramatic cliffs

activities.name: Arrive Takayama Station
Check in Residence Hotel — 30 SECONDS from station!
activities.description: Night 1 of 2 — full apartment with kitchen!

activities.name: Sanmachi Suji historic district + sake tastings
activities.description: Edo-period merchant lanes — look for sugidama (cedar ball) above breweries = open for tasting!

activities.name: Takayama Jinya — only surviving Edo government house
activities.description: The only remaining feudal government office in Japan — beautifully preserved

activities.name: Higashiyama Temple Walk — hilltop Takayama views
activities.description: Walk along the temple trail above Takayama for panoramic views at golden hour

activities.name: Dinner — Hida beef ramen or Hida beef sukiyaki
activities.description: Hida beef is world-class Wagyu raised in the Hida mountains — don't miss it!

activities.name: Sanmachi Suji at night — lanterns and silence
activities.description: One of Japan's most beautiful quiet experiences. Lanterns glowing in the historic lanes.

tips: Wide View Hida: reserve LEFT side A/B seats at any JR office for best Hida River gorge views.
Sake tip: look for a sugidama (cedar ball) hanging above brewery entrances — sign they are open for tasting.
Sanmachi Suji at night is one of Japan's most beautiful quiet experiences.

itinerary.title: Morning Market + Hida Folk Village + Cedar Avenue + Sanmachi Suji

activities.name: Breakfast — Lawson near Takayama Station
activities.description: Near hotel

activities.name: Miyagawa Morning Market — running since Edo period!
activities.description: 7 AM sharp for freshest produce and quietest atmosphere. Bring cash — most stalls cash only!

activities.name: Collect large bags at hotel (arrived via TA-Q-BIN)
activities.description: Bags sent from Osaka — now you have everything again!

activities.name: Morning Sanmachi Suji — mist sometimes, quiet lanes
activities.description: The morning atmosphere is magical — locals going about their day

activities.name: Kusakabe Folk Museum — 1879 preserved merchant house
activities.description: Step inside a perfectly preserved Meiji-era merchant family home

activities.name: Cedar Avenue (Sugi-namiki) — 360-year-old cryptomeria
activities.description: Ancient cedar trees lining the road — almost no tourists! Peaceful and ancient.

activities.name: Lunch at Amazake-chaya — 400yr old thatched teahouse
activities.description: Open since 1638! Try amazake (sweet fermented rice drink) in the 400-year-old thatched building

activities.name: Taxi to Hida Folk Village
activities.description: 10 min taxi

activities.name: Hida Folk Village — 30+ gassho-zukuri farmhouses
activities.description: Open-air museum with massive thatched farmhouses relocated from around the Hida region. Walk INSIDE every house!

activities.name: Taxi back toward Sanmachi Suji
activities.description: 10 min taxi

activities.name: Final Sanmachi — sake shopping, crafts, Hida beef manju
activities.description: Last chance for sake shopping! Buy direct from Funasaka, Ota or Hirase breweries.

activities.name: Dinner — Hida beef sukiyaki + hoba miso + local sake
activities.description: Hoba miso: miso paste grilled on a magnolia leaf — Takayama specialty!

activities.name: Final Takayama night walk — lanterns + total silence
activities.description: One of the most beautiful quiet experiences in Japan. Unforgettable.

activities.name: Back to hotel — pack for Tokyo tomorrow
activities.description: Set alarm for 7:00 AM!

tips: Morning Market: 7 AM sharp for freshest produce and quietest atmosphere. Cash only!
Buy sake DIRECT from Funasaka, Ota or Hirase breweries — namazake (unpasteurized sake) is the local specialty unavailable outside Hida region.
Hida Folk Village: allow 1.5–2 hrs minimum — walk inside every farmhouse.

itinerary.title: Morning Market → Wide View Hida → Akihabara + A-PIT → Tokyo

activities.name: Breakfast — convenience store near station
activities.description: Near hotel

activities.name: Miyagawa Morning Market — final farewell!
activities.description: Different vendors daily — last chance for Takayama!

activities.name: Check out Residence Hotel
activities.description: 30 SECOND walk to station!

activities.name: Wide View Hida — Takayama → Nagoya
activities.description: LEFT side for gorge views on return journey too

activities.name: Arrive Nagoya — Hitsumabushi eel rice ekiben lunch
activities.description: Nagoya specialty: eel rice eaten three ways! Station platform shops.

activities.name: Reserve Green Car at JR Midori-no-madoguchi
activities.description: Show JR Pass, pay Green Car supplement. RIGHT side D/E seats for final Mt Fuji view!

activities.name: 🌿 GREEN CAR Hikari Shinkansen — Nagoya → Tokyo
activities.description: RIGHT side D/E seats! FINAL Mt Fuji view around 12:20 PM between Shin-Fuji and Mishima stations. Wider seats, more legroom, quieter cabin.

activities.name: 🗻 FINAL Mt Fuji view from the train!
activities.description: Between Shin-Fuji and Mishima stations — RIGHT side seats — very last Fuji sighting of the entire trip!

activities.name: Arrive Tokyo Station — welcome back to Tokyo!
JR Chuo Line — Tokyo → Shinjuku
activities.description: 30 min

activities.name: Store bags at Shinjuku Station coin lockers
activities.description: Central concourse

activities.name: JR to Akihabara
activities.description: 25 min via Ochanomizu

activities.name: Akihabara — Yodobashi Akiba + Super Potato + Animate
activities.description: Electronics megastore, retro game shop, anime goods — Akiba in full glory!

activities.name: Rinkai Line → Shinonome
activities.description: 30 min (change at Osaki)

activities.name: 🚗 A-PIT Super Autobacs — JDM paradise! 4 floors!
activities.description: Walk the parking lot first for show cars, then explore 4 floors of JDM parts, merch, and goods. Show cars change regularly!

activities.name: Rinkai Line + JR Yamanote → Shinjuku
activities.description: 35 min

activities.name: Collect bags from coin lockers
activities.description: Shinjuku Station central concourse

activities.name: Check in Yuen Shinjuku
activities.description: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station — night 1 of 2

activities.name: Dinner — Tsunahachi tempura (since 1923)
activities.description: Classic old-school Tokyo tempura restaurant — been frying since 1923!

activities.name: Omoide Yokocho — welcome back to Tokyo, full circle!
activities.description: Back to where it all started on May 12! Nostalgic return.

activities.name: ♨️ ONSEN 6 — Yuen Shinjuku rooftop onsen
activities.description: Indoor + outdoor with the Shinjuku skyline! Best around 11 PM when quieter.

tips: Final Fuji view: RIGHT side Green Car seats, around 12:20 PM between Shin-Fuji and Mishima stations — very last Fuji sighting of the trip!
A-PIT parking lot first — walk the lot before going inside. Show cars change regularly.
Yuen Shinjuku onsen around 11 PM when quieter — Shinjuku skyline at night from outdoor hot spring is unforgettable.

itinerary.title: Harajuku + Cat Street + Isetan + Shinjuku Shopping

activities.name: Depart Yuen Shinjuku
JR Yamanote to Harajuku
activities.description: 7 min

activities.name: Harajuku — Takeshita Street kawaii fashion and crepes
activities.description: Wild street fashion, colourful crepes, Japanese pop culture

activities.name: Cat Street — Off-White, Supreme Japan, BAPE, Neighborhood
activities.description: Supreme Japan has Japan-only releases! BAPE Harajuku has Japan-only colorways!

activities.name: Lunch in Harajuku or Omotesando area
Ura-Harajuku boutiques — Sacai, Kapital, Nanamica, Beams Japan
activities.description: Beams Japan is the best curated selection of Japanese brands and folk crafts under one roof!

activities.name: JR Yamanote to Shinjuku
activities.description: 7 min

activities.name: Isetan Shinjuku — food hall + craft floor + menswear
activities.description: Japan's finest department store. B1-B2 food hall is legendary. 6F craft goods.

activities.name: Shinjuku area — Tokyu Hands + Don Quijote
activities.description: Tokyu Hands: best selection of Japanese design goods, stationery, tools

activities.name: Dinner — New York Bar Park Hyatt or Kozue
activities.description: Splurge on the last real dinner! New York Bar from Lost in Translation, or Kozue for Japanese-French
activities.map_query: New York Bar Park Hyatt Tokyo Shinjuku

activities.name: Omoide Yokocho — final visit
activities.description: 2 min from hotel

activities.name: ♨️ Yuen Shinjuku rooftop onsen — enjoy the skyline!
activities.description: In hotel

tips: Cat Street: Supreme Japan has Japan-only releases — different stock from US stores! BAPE Harajuku original flagship has Japan-only colorways.
Beams Japan is the best curated selection of Japanese brands and folk crafts under one roof.
Ask every store for tax-free shopping — show your passport for 10% refund on purchases over ¥5,000.

itinerary.title: Takashimaya + Final Shopping → Narita → Fly Home

activities.name: Wake up — final morning in Japan
Breakfast at Yuen Shinjuku
activities.description: Hotel restaurant

activities.name: Check out — store bags at front desk
activities.description: Store bags until 1:50 PM

activities.name: Walk to Takashimaya Times Square
activities.description: 8 min walk from hotel

activities.name: Takashimaya Times Square — final souvenir mission!
activities.description: B1–B2: Kit Kats, wagashi, sake, matcha, yuzu | 6F: ceramics, lacquerware, washi, knives | 8F: tax-free counter

activities.name: Shinjuku final shopping — Beams Japan + Tokyu Hands
activities.description: Last chance for Japanese goods!

activities.name: Final Tokyo lunch — Afuri Ramen or Tsunahachi tempura
activities.description: Last meal in Japan — make it count!

activities.name: Collect bags from Yuen Shinjuku
activities.description: 8 min walk

activities.name: ⚠️ N'EX Narita Express — Shinjuku → Narita Airport
activities.description: DO NOT MISS THIS TRAIN — 90 min last train ride in Japan!

activities.name: Arrive Narita Airport — check in + bags + security
activities.description: Allow 3 hours before departure — do NOT cut this short!

activities.name: Duty free — Japanese whisky + cosmetics + snacks
activities.description: After security in departure terminal. Yamazaki, Hibiki, Nikka whisky — best prices in Japan!

activities.name: Board flight home — Narita (NRT) → Austin (AUS)
activities.description: Connection through LAX or DFW. Sayonara, Japan! 🇯🇵

tips: Tax-free shopping: show passport at EVERY store — 10% refund on purchases over ¥5,000.
Whisky: Yamazaki and Hibiki often sold out in shops — Narita duty free usually has good stock.
Return Suica cards at Narita JR office to get remaining balance plus ¥500 deposit refund per card.

bookings_checklist.item: JR Pass × 2 (14-day) — MUST BUY OUTSIDE JAPAN
bookings_checklist.where_to_book: Travel agency or online before departing Austin
bookings_checklist.notes: Cannot buy in Japan! Purchase before you leave Austin.
Sells out weeks ahead! Book immediately.
May Natsu Basho runs May 11–25. Book now — sell out!
Book at least 2 weeks ahead. Seat 1A or 1B for panoramic front view.

bookings_checklist.item: Kimono Rental Wargo — Asakusa branch — May 13

bookings_checklist.notes: Book 10:30 AM slot for best Mt Fuji visibility.

bookings_checklist.where_to_book: Any JR office on arrival in Japan
bookings_checklist.notes: Reserve immediately on arrival in Japan — only 18 seats per train!

bookings_checklist.where_to_book: Nagoya Station JR office on May 23
bookings_checklist.notes: Reserve at Nagoya on May 23 morning.

experiences_checklist.item: Kimono dress-up at Wargo Asakusa
experiences_checklist.description: Transform into kimono for a day in Tokyo!

experiences_checklist.item: Sumo Natsu Basho — top division bouts
experiences_checklist.description: Watch Makuuchi bouts at Ryogoku Kokugikan. Yokozuna bout ~5:45 PM!

experiences_checklist.item: teamLab Planets immersive digital art
experiences_checklist.description: Walk through water rooms and infinity mirrors in Toyosu

experiences_checklist.item: Odakyu Romance Car scenic mountain train
experiences_checklist.description: 85 min mountain journey to Hakone — RIGHT side for Mt Fuji!

experiences_checklist.item: Mt Fuji view from Chureito Pagoda
experiences_checklist.description: The most iconic Japan photo — five-story pagoda framing Mt Fuji

experiences_checklist.item: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway + ring the love bell
experiences_checklist.description: Cable car above Lake Kawaguchi, ring the summit bell together

experiences_checklist.item: Hakone Open Air Museum foot onsen
experiences_checklist.description: Soak feet while looking at outdoor sculptures and Hakone mountains

experiences_checklist.item: Gran Class Shinkansen — Odawara to Kyoto
experiences_checklist.description: Fully reclining seats, meal, drinks, hot towel, amenity kit

experiences_checklist.item: Green Car Shinkansen — Nagoya to Tokyo
experiences_checklist.description: Wide seats, quiet cabin, final Mt Fuji view at 12:20 PM

experiences_checklist.item: Fujiya Hotel historic mineral onsen (×3 soaks)
experiences_checklist.description: Mountain mineral baths in Japan's most historic hotel

experiences_checklist.item: Authentic matcha tea ceremony — 90 min
experiences_checklist.description: Full English tea ceremony at Camellia, Kyoto

experiences_checklist.item: Samurai & Ninja Museum — armor + sword + shuriken
experiences_checklist.description: Try on real samurai armor, handle a katana, throw shuriken!

experiences_checklist.item: Fushimi Inari — thousand torii gates
experiences_checklist.description: Climb PAST Yotsutsuji for the best upper section (most tourists stop early)

experiences_checklist.item: Kinkaku-ji the Golden Pavilion
experiences_checklist.description: Temple covered in actual gold leaf — afternoon light makes it shimmer

experiences_checklist.item: Gion Hanamikoji Street — spot a maiko
experiences_checklist.description: 6–7:30 PM, walk slowly, no flash photography, bow respectfully

experiences_checklist.item: Osaka Castle — 8 floors + observation deck
experiences_checklist.description: Giant stone walls, museum, gold tigers, 360° city views

experiences_checklist.item: Dotonbori neon canal + Glico Man couple photo
experiences_checklist.description: Stand on Ebisu Bridge at dusk facing west — mandatory!

experiences_checklist.item: Sanmachi Suji Takayama Edo-period lanes (day + night)
experiences_checklist.description: Historic merchant district — lanterns at night are magical

experiences_checklist.item: Hida Folk Village — gassho-zukuri farmhouses
experiences_checklist.description: 30+ massive thatched farmhouses — walk inside every one!

experiences_checklist.item: Miyagawa Morning Market — Takayama
experiences_checklist.description: Running since the Edo period — go at 7 AM for the best experience

experiences_checklist.item: A-PIT Super Autobacs — JDM paradise!
experiences_checklist.description: 4 floors of JDM glory in Shinonome, Tokyo — walk the parking lot first

experiences_checklist.item: Harajuku + Cat Street streetwear
experiences_checklist.description: Japan-only Supreme, BAPE flagship, Beams Japan, Ura-Harajuku boutiques

experiences_checklist.item: Takashimaya Times Square food hall + craft floor
experiences_checklist.description: Final souvenir mission — B1 food hall, 6F crafts, 8F tax-free counter

experiences_checklist.item: Narita duty free — Japanese whisky haul
experiences_checklist.description: Yamazaki, Hibiki, Nikka — best prices and often better stock than shops
